About: It is about Jesus Christ as Pantocrator.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Byzantine art.
It is associated with Eastern Orthodox Christianity.
It is associated with Eastern Catholic Christianity.
CreatedIn: It was created in the Byzantine capital of Constantinople.
HasA: It has a height of 84 cm.
It has a width of 45.5 cm.
It has a thickness of 1.2 cm.
It has a large portion of Christ's hair on his left side with damage.
It has a Gospel book clutched in the left hand and arm.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by the dual nature of Christ.
It is characterized by traits of both man and God.
It is characterized by features on the right side representing human nature.
It is characterized by features on the left side representing divinity.
It is characterized by a right hand shown opening outward signifying blessing.
It is characterized by encaustic medium using hot wax paint.
HeldIn: It was held in Saint Catherine's Monastery.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the aftermath of the ecumenical councils of the previous century at Ephesus and Chalcedon.
IsA: It is one of the oldest Byzantine religious icons.
It is the earliest known surviving depiction of Jesus Christ as Pantocrator.
It is regarded by historians and scholars among the most important and recognizable works in the study of Byzantine art.
It is a possible representation of the Kamouliana icon of Christ.
It is a possible representation of the famous icon of Christ of the Chalke Gate.
KnownAs: It is known as Christ Pantocrator of Saint Catherine's Monastery.
KnownFor: It is known for being one of the oldest Byzantine religious icons.
It is known for being the earliest known surviving depiction of Jesus Christ as Pantocrator.
It is known for its importance and recognizability in the study of Byzantine art.
It is known for representing the dual nature of Christ.
LocatedIn: It is located in Saint Catherine's Monastery.
OccurredIn: It occurred in the 6th century AD.
It occurred in the mid-sixth century.
It occurred in the Byzantine capital of Constantinople.
It occurred in the southern Levant.
It occurred in the era of iconoclasm initiated by Emperor Leo III.
It occurred in the eighth and ninth centuries.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the Byzantine capital of Constantinople.
UsedFor: It is used for the study of Byzantine art.
It is used for the study of Eastern Orthodox Christianity.
It is used for the study of Eastern Catholic Christianity.
UsedIn: It is used in Byzantine art.
It is used in Eastern Orthodox Christianity.
It is used in Eastern Catholic Christianity.
